title: AVIATION — full history of Iranian civil aviation 1913-2007, incl. Iran Air founding and fleet/workforce growth
source_org: Encyclopaedia Iranica (Encyclopaedia Iranica Foundation), article by Abbas Atrvash
time_coverage: 1913-2007 (pre-Iran-Air era 1913-1962; Iran Air/HOMA state-airline era 1962-1979; post-revolution era 1979-2007)
frequency: one-off reference article with embedded dated events and a few quantitative snapshots
units: described per-row in CSV; rials, persons, aircraft counts
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica — academic reference, cite author/article per their citation guidance
notes: Directly addresses the aviation topic's pre-1970 WDI gap: covers Iran Air's February 1962 founding (170M rial startup capital, absorbing Iranian Airways + Persian Air Services), the predecessor private airlines back to 1944 (Iranian Airways) and even the 1927 Junkers airmail service and 1921-23 Air Force founding. Workforce grew from 700 (1962) to over 12,000 (by end of the 1962-1978 Khademi era). Fleet reached 35 all-jet aircraft (29 Boeing + 6 Airbus) carrying 'close to five million passengers a year' by approximately 1978 (exact year not stated in source, flagged as such in both .txt and .csv). Also documents the sharp post-1979 institutional decline (executive turnover, US sanctions crippling maintenance, the 1988 Flight 655 shootdown killing 290) -- a genuine 'before and after' aviation story parallel to the cinema and telecom findings elsewhere in this hunt. Retrieved via interactive browser tool (get_page_text) since iranicaonline.org blocks curl/WebFetch with Cloudflare (HTTP 403), consistent with every other retrieval from this domain in this database. Companion dataset encyclopedia-com-iranair-company-history/ (sibling folder) provides a denser year-by-year passenger/employee/revenue series that complements this article's narrative-embedded figures.

failures.method: curl (not separately attempted; domain confirmed Cloudflare-blocked repeatedly this session)
failures.result: not attempted, known-blocked domain, used browser tool directly
